stemHtml: Yaw is followed by roll because the:

options.a: rudder is located above the longitudinal axis and when it is deflected, it causes a rolling moment in the same direction as the yaw.
options.b: rolling motion generated by rudder deflection causes a speed increase of the outer wing which increases the lift on that wing so the aeroplane starts to roll in the direction of the turn.
options.c: yawing motion generated by rudder deflection causes a speed increase of the inner wing, which increase the lift on that wing so that the aeroplane starts to roll in the same direction as the yaw.
options.d: yawing motion generated by rudder deflection causes a speed increase of the outer wing, which increases the lift on that wing so that the aeroplane starts to roll in the same direction as the yaw.
